areas.title: Data-Centric AI
areas.short: Advancing AI by improving the data it learns from — through better curation, quality assessment, diversity analysis, and rigorous benchmarking for clinical reliability.
areas.description: Advancing AI by improving the data it learns from — through better curation, quality assessment, diversity analysis, and rigorous benchmarking for clinical reliability.

areas.title: Intelligent Clinical Tools
areas.short: Designing AI-powered tools that integrate meaningfully into clinical workflows — reducing burden, supporting decision-making, and adapting to real-world healthcare settings.
areas.description: Designing AI-powered tools that integrate meaningfully into clinical workflows — reducing burden, supporting decision-making, and adapting to real-world healthcare settings.

areas.title: Simulation & Virtual Trials
areas.short: Leveraging physics-based simulation and virtual patient models to evaluate AI rigorously, safely, and at scale — where real-world clinical testing alone is limited or costly.
areas.description: Leveraging physics-based simulation and virtual patient models to evaluate AI rigorously, safely, and at scale — where real-world clinical testing alone is limited or costly.

areas.title: Synthetic & Generative Data
areas.short: Creating controllable, privacy-preserving synthetic datasets and generative models for AI training, evaluation, and reproducible experimentation in medicine.
areas.description: Creating controllable, privacy-preserving synthetic datasets and generative models for AI training, evaluation, and reproducible experimentation in medicine.

featured.title: HAID
featured.heading: Health AI Data Resource
featured.summary: An open-access repository providing 13+ standardized medical imaging datasets covering lung cancer, COVID-19, and universal lesions with expert-verified annotations, preprocessed files, and train-test splits to democratize access to high-quality clinical data for AI/ML development.
featured.impact: Democratizes access to high-quality, standardized clinical data — accelerating reproducible AI research across multiple disease domains.

featured.title: TriAnnot
featured.heading: Multi-Stage AI Pipeline for Lung Nodule Annotation
featured.summary: A comprehensive, freely available pipeline integrating lung segmentation, nodule detection, and malignancy classification into a unified tri-stage workflow designed to prioritize sensitivity while reducing annotator burden for first-pass lung nodule annotation in screening CT.
featured.impact: Reduces manual annotation burden and improves consistency in lung nodule identification — enabling faster, more reliable screening workflows.

projects.title: VLST
projects.heading: Virtual Lung Screening Trial
projects.description: A large-scale virtual imaging trial simulating low-dose CT lung cancer screening across diverse patient populations, scanner models, and acquisition protocols.

projects.title: AI in Lung Health
projects.heading: Benchmarking Detection and Diagnostic Models Across Multiple CT Scan Datasets
projects.description: A public, reproducible multi-dataset benchmark for lung nodule detection and cancer classification, demonstrating that detection performance is strongly driven by dataset characteristics.

projects.title: NoMAISI
projects.heading: Nodule-Oriented Medical AI for Synthetic Imaging
projects.description: A generative framework using flow-based diffusion and ControlNet conditioning to create realistic synthetic CT images with AI-generated lung nodules for data augmentation.

projects.title: HAID
projects.heading: Health AI Data Resource
projects.description: An open-access repository providing 13+ standardized medical imaging datasets with expert-verified annotations and preprocessed files for AI/ML development.

projects.title: TriAnnot
projects.heading: Multi-Stage AI Pipeline for Lung Nodule Annotation
projects.description: A freely available pipeline integrating lung segmentation, nodule detection, and malignancy classification into a unified tri-stage workflow for screening CT.
